'''Vincent Duport''' (born 15 December 1987) is a French professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]]. He was previously playing for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Co-operative Championship]]. Primarily a centre but also used to playing in the back row and wing. In his early career, he was highly regarded in France and was one of the “ players to watch “ for [[France]] in the 2008 [[Rugby League World Cup]].

==Background==
Duport was born in [[Toulouse]], [[Midi-Pyrénées]], [[France]].

==Career==
He was named in the [[France national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Squad|France training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="France dominated by Dragons">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949078,00.html|title=France dominated by Dragons|publisher=Sky Sports|date=7 August 2008|access-date=8 August 2008}}</ref>

Duport returned to the Catalans Dragons for the remainder of the 2010 season after being granted a release from Toulouse.<ref>http://www.sportinglife.com/rugbyleague/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=rleague/10/07/01/RUGBYL_Catalans.html {{Dead link|date=February 2022}}</ref>

On 22 October 2016, Duport returned to international rugby league for the first time since the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]]. Duport was part of the French team that took on England in an [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations#France vs England|end of year test match]] in Avignon.
